name: Pop Goes the Weasel
source: ABC notation from thesession.org/tunes/10534 and abcnotation.com (Traditional Tune Archive). The melody is traditionally played in G major in 6/8 time.
notes: Traditional English nursery rhyme and jig from the mid-19th century. The 'Pop!' occurs on the E5 (measure 7). This version represents the main vocal melody in G major. The song is often played at a lively tempo (120 BPM) with a bouncy 6/8 feel. The rhythmic values have been simplified to quarter and eighth notes for playback - in authentic 6/8 time, some notes would be dotted quarters (3 eighth notes). Lyrics: 'All around the mulberry bush, the monkey chased the weasel. The monkey thought 'twas all in fun, Pop! Goes the weasel.'
